steps_raw.instruction: Add 770 uL of Trizol to bead bashing tube (1 per sample).
Pour tissue into a clean Petri dish, keep on cooling block (flat ice pack).

Transfer pieces of tissue to bead bashing tube, blot RNAlater with a kim tissue. May be a very small amount of tissue (if so use all) and blot until it’s REALLY dry. Do all of this on top of an ice pack to keep it cold.
Beadbash 3-4 times for 15 sec at max speed. Cool down 5-10 seconds in between, placing tube in/on ice block. Stop as soon as tissue is dissolved!
Transfer liquid to a 1.6 mL RNase free tube and centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Use block from lab freezer to keep tubes cold.
Transfer supernatant to a new 1.6 mL RNase free tube.
Add 700 uL of 100% ethanol to each tube. Vortex.
1) Discard flow-through. Repeat with remaining 700 uL (if applicable). If first half did not flow through completely, centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 2 minutes .
2) Transfer column to RNase-free tube and do in-column digestion. Wash with 400 uL of RNA Wash Buffer . Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 30 seconds , discard flow-through.

3) Add 80 uL of DNase I Reaction Mix ( 75 µL, DNA digestion Buffer (kit), 5 µL, DNase I (freezer, green cap); scaled) directly to the column. Incubate for 00:15:00, at room temperature . Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 30 seconds .
4) Add 400 uL of Direct-zol RNA PreWash . Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
5) Discard flow through. Repeat previous step. (Pre-wash and centrifuge again).
6) Add 700 uL of RNA Wash Buffer . Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through. Centrifuge again in emptied collection tube 12000 x g, °C, 2 minutes .
7) Transfer column to labeled RNase free tube.
8) Add 26 uL of RNase-free water to column. Incubate 00:03:00, at room temperature . Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute .
9) Combine tubes if applicable (see note on step 8). Take 5 uL for QC/Qubit. The kit is called “Qubit RNA HS” and has weirdly clear dye and different standards.

Load 700 uL into ZymoSpin IIC Column in the 2mL-size RNase-free collection tube. Use this size for all remaining steps. Centrifuge 12000 x g, °C, 1 minute . For sea cucumbers, urchins, or other tissues that might clog the filter, split them into two ZymoSpin IIC Columns instead of doing step 9: i.e., treat like two "separate" samples for the rest of the protocol, then combine them at the end.
